strategies.name: Update CHECK lines for Loop Size in store_cost.ll
strategies.rationale: The patch removes the indentation from `LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Loop Size = " << LoopSize << "\n");` in `tryToUnrollLoop`. Previously it was `LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "  Loop Size = " << LoopSize << "\n");`. The test `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopUnroll/X86/store_cost.ll` has a CHECK line `CHECK:   Loop Size = 25` which expects the indentation. This test will fail after the patch.
strategies.expected_issue: The test will fail because the output of `Loop Size = ` is no longer indented by 2 spaces, but the CHECK line expects `  Loop Size = 25`.

strategies.name: Update CHECK lines for Loop Size in gh-issue77118-broken-lcssa-form.ll
strategies.rationale: The patch removes the indentation from `LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Loop Size = " << LoopSize << "\n");`. The test `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopUnroll/gh-issue77118-broken-lcssa-form.ll` has CHECK lines like `CHECK-NEXT:    Loop Size = 3` which expect the indentation. This test will fail after the patch.
strategies.expected_issue: The test will fail because the output of `Loop Size = ` is no longer indented by 4 spaces, but the CHECK line expects `    Loop Size = 3`.

strategies.name: Update CHECK lines for Loop Size in guard-cost-for-unrolling.ll
strategies.rationale: The patch removes the indentation from `LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Loop Size = " << LoopSize << "\n");`. The test `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopUnroll/guard-cost-for-unrolling.ll` has CHECK lines like `CHECK-NEXT:    Loop Size = 8` which expect the indentation. This test will fail after the patch.
strategies.expected_issue: The test will fail because the output of `Loop Size = ` is no longer indented by 4 spaces, but the CHECK line expects `    Loop Size = 8`.

strategies.name: Update CHECK lines for Loop Size in loop-micro-op-buffer-size-t99.ll
strategies.rationale: The patch removes the indentation from `LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Loop Size = " << LoopSize << "\n");`. The test `llvm/test/CodeGen/AArch64/loop-micro-op-buffer-size-t99.ll` has CHECK lines like `CHECK:   Loop Size = 18` which expect the indentation. This test will fail after the patch.
strategies.expected_issue: The test will fail because the output of `Loop Size = ` is no longer indented by 2 spaces, but the CHECK line expects `  Loop Size = 18`.

reason_thou:
1. "Fix Understanding": The patch modifies the `LLVM_DEBUG` messages in `LoopUnrollPass.cpp` to use `dbgs().indent(N)` instead of hardcoded spaces for indentation. This unifies the ad-hoc use of whitespace and makes it easier to see which loop debug messages correspond to which part of the loop unrolling heuristics. However, for the message `"Loop Size = "`, the patch changes `LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "  Loop Size = " << LoopSize << "\n");` to `LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Loop Size = " << LoopSize << "\n");`, completely removing the indentation.

2. "Formal Semantic Modeling": The semantic change is purely in the debug output format. Before the fix, the debug output for loop size was `  Loop Size = X`. After the fix, it is `Loop Size = X`.

3. "Assumptions Identified": The patch assumes that changing the debug output format will not break any tests, or that the author has updated all corresponding tests. However, many tests in LLVM rely on exact string matching for debug output using `FileCheck`.

4. "Potential Cases to Break Assumptions": Any test that checks for the exact string `  Loop Size = ` or `    Loop Size = ` (depending on how `FileCheck` matches it, sometimes with `CHECK-NEXT` it matches the exact indentation) will fail. I have identified several tests that do this:
- `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopUnroll/X86/store_cost.ll`
- `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopUnroll/gh-issue77118-broken-lcssa-form.ll`
- `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopUnroll/guard-cost-for-unrolling.ll`
- `llvm/test/CodeGen/AArch64/loop-micro-op-buffer-size-t99.ll`

5. "Test Strategies": The strategy is to point out these specific tests that will fail due to the changed debug output format. The tests need to be updated to match the new format without the leading spaces.
report:
{
  "test": [
    "```llvm
; RUN: opt -mcpu=core-avx2 -passes=loop-unroll -debug-only=loop-unroll -S -unroll-allow-partial < %s 2>&1 | FileCheck %s

target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

; CHECK: Loop Unroll: F[foo] Loop %loop.header
; CHECK: Loop Size = 25
; CHECK: UNROLLING loop %loop.header by 2 
; CHECK: Loop Unroll: F[foo] Loop %loop.2.header
; CHECK: Loop Size = 27
; CHECK-NOT: UNROLLING loop %loop.2.header

define void @foo(ptr %out) {
entry:
  %0 = alloca [1024 x i32]
  %x0 = alloca [1024 x i32]
  %x01 = alloca [1024 x i32]
  %x02 = alloca [1024 x i32]
  %x03 = alloca [1024 x i32]
  %x04 = alloca [1024 x i32]
  %x05 = alloca [1024 x i32]
  %x06 = alloca [1024 x i32]
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:
  %counter = phi i32 [0, %entry], [%inc, %loop.inc]
  br label %loop.body

loop.body:
  %ptr = getelementptr [1024 x i32], ptr %0, i32 0, i32 %counter
  store i32 %counter, ptr %ptr
  %val = add i32 %counter, 5
  %xptr = getelementptr [1024 x i32], ptr %x0, i32 0, i32 %counter
  store i32 %val, ptr %xptr
  %val1 = add i32 %counter, 6
  %xptr1 = getelementptr [1024 x i32], ptr %x01, i32 0, i32 %counter
  store i32 %val1, ptr %xptr1
  %val2 = add i32 %counter, 7
  %xptr2 = getelementptr [1024 x i32], ptr %x02, i32 0, i32 %counter
  store i32 %val2, ptr %xptr2
  %val3 = add i32 %counter, 8
  %xptr3 = getelementptr [1024 x i32], ptr %x03, i32 0, i32 %counter
  store i32 %val3, ptr %xptr3
  %val4 = add i32 %counter, 9
  %xptr4 = getelementptr [1024 x i32], ptr %x04, i32 0, i32 %counter
  store i32 %val4, ptr %xptr4
  %val5 = add i32 %counter, 10
  %xptr5 = getelementptr [1024 x i32], ptr %x05, i32 0, i32 %counter
  store i32 %val5, ptr %xptr5
  br label %loop.inc

loop.inc:
  %inc = add i32 %counter, 2
  %1 = icmp sge i32 %inc, 1023
  br i1 %1, label  %exit.0, label %loop.header

exit.0:
  %2 = getelementptr [1024 x i32], ptr %0, i32 0, i32 5
  %3 = load i32, ptr %2
  store i32 %3, ptr %out
  br label %loop.2.header


loop.2.header:
  %counter.2 = phi i32 [0, %exit.0], [%inc.2, %loop.2.inc]
  br label %loop.2.body

loop.2.body:
  %ptr.2 = getelementptr [1024 x i32], ptr %0, i32 0, i32 %counter.2
  store i32 %counter.2, ptr %ptr.2
  %val.2 = add i32 %counter.2, 5
  %xptr.2 = getelementptr [1024 x i32], ptr %x0, i32 0, i32 %counter.2
  store i32 %val.2, ptr %xptr.2
  %val1.2 = add i32 %counter.2, 6
  %xptr1.2 = getelementptr [1024 x i32], ptr %x01, i32 0, i32 %counter.2
  store i32 %val1, ptr %xptr1.2
  %val2.2 = add i32 %counter.2, 7
  %xptr2.2 = getelementptr [1024 x i32], ptr %x02, i32 0, i32 %counter.2
  store i32 %val2, ptr %xptr2.2
  %val3.2 = add i32 %counter.2, 8
  %xptr3.2 = getelementptr [1024 x i32], ptr %x03, i32 0, i32 %counter.2
  store i32 %val3.2, ptr %xptr3.2
  %val4.2 = add i32 %counter.2, 9
  %xptr4.2 = getelementptr [1024 x i32], ptr %x04, i32 0, i32 %counter.2
  store i32 %val4.2, ptr %xptr4.2
  %val5.2 = add i32 %counter.2, 10
  %xptr5.2 = getelementptr [1024 x i32], ptr %x05, i32 0, i32 %counter.2
  store i32 %val5.2, ptr %xptr5.2
  %xptr6.2 = getelementptr [1024 x i32], ptr %x06, i32 0, i32 %counter.2
  store i32 %val5.2, ptr %xptr6.2
  br label %loop.2.inc

loop.2.inc:
  %inc.2 = add i32 %counter.2, 2
  %4 = icmp sge i32 %inc.2, 1023
  br i1 %4, label  %exit.2, label %loop.2.header

exit.2:
  %x2 = getelementptr [1024 x i32], ptr %0, i32 0, i32 6
  %x3 = load i32, ptr %x2
  %out2 = getelementptr i32, ptr %out, i32 1
  store i32 %3, ptr %out2
  ret void
}
```",
    "opt -mcpu=core-avx2 -passes=loop-unroll -debug-only=loop-unroll -S -unroll-allow-partial < %s 2>&1 | FileCheck %s"
  ],
  "thoughts": "The patch changes the debug output format for `Loop Size = ` by removing the indentation. This breaks several tests that rely on exact string matching for debug output using `FileCheck`. I have identified the tests and updated the CHECK lines to match the new format.",
  "force": true
}
